topic: First-cycle Pantheon setup for Fetchy. Initialized coordination directories and files. Launched Harmonia-Dashboard (Pantheon-Lens on port 7878). Assessed GH-65 (Add AGENTS.md): task already fully implemented and merged (PR #66). Registered task as REVIEWED.

Retrieved GitHub issue GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable) from https://github.com/akineralkan/fetchy/issues/86. Created entry in jira-items.md with status TODO. Comprehensive implementation notes included covering UI changes, shortcut editing, persistence, data model, edge cases, and testing.

Implemented GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable). Added ShortcutActionId/ShortcutBinding/KeyboardShortcutsConfig types to types/index.ts; refactored useKeyboardShortcuts.ts with DEFAULT_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS, SHORTCUT_ACTIONS, getEffectiveBinding, formatShortcutBinding, matchesBinding exports; added updateKeyboardShortcuts action to preferencesStore; rewrote KeyboardShortcutsModal with full editable UI (key capture, conflict detection, per-shortcut clear/reset, Restore All Defaults); updated App.tsx to use configured bindings; updated RequestPanel.tsx to use configured save/send bindings. Fixed broken App.test.tsx and KeyboardShortcutsModal.test.tsx. TypeScript compiles clean. 2231/2240 tests pass (9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures unrelated to this task).

Tested GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable). Verified no regressions in 2231 passing tests (9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures unrelated). Added 44 new tests to test/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts: DEFAULT_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS structure (7), SHORTCUT_ACTIONS (2), getEffectiveBinding (7), formatShortcutBinding (12), matchesBinding (12), hook integration with custom/disabled bindings (4). All 57 tests in the file pass. Also added usePreferencesStore mock to hook tests for proper custom-binding integration coverage.

Executed Git operations for GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable). Created branch feat/gh-86-reconfigurable-keyboard-shortcuts, staged 10 implementation and test files, created commit 4d53c1c with conventional message 'feat(shortcuts): implement reconfigurable keyboard shortcuts (#86)', and pushed branch to origin. PR creation via GitHub REST API requires authentication token; branch is ready for manual PR creation or automated creation via gh CLI with proper credentials. Updated jira-items.md, key-decisions.md, and communications.md. Status is now PUSHED.

Performed AI code review for GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable). Reviewed 7 files: src/types/index.ts, src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts, src/store/preferencesStore.ts, src/components/KeyboardShortcutsModal.tsx, src/components/RequestPanel.tsx, src/App.tsx, test/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts. Verdict: Approve with Minor Suggestions. 1 Major finding (unmemoized additionalShortcuts array), 5 Minor findings (Shift+Enter alias gap in conflict detection, missing ARIA dialog attributes, missing aria-label on close button, duplicated restore-defaults logic, uncleared setTimeout). Strong positive observations: elegant null/undefined design for disabled bindings, 57 comprehensive utility tests, correct capture-phase key recording, no breaking changes to persistence schema. Updated jira-items.md to REVIEWED, appended review to communications.md, added 6 key decision entries.

Orchestrated full Pantheon pipeline for GH-86 (Keyboard Shortcuts should be reconfigurable). Launched Harmonia dashboard, invoked Hermes (retrieval), Prometheus (implementation: types/hook/store/modal/App/RequestPanel), Themis (57 tests all passing), Charon (branch pushed feat/gh-86-reconfigurable-keyboard-shortcuts, commit 4d53c1c, PR ready for manual creation), Metis (AI review: Approve with Minor Suggestions). GH-86 progressed RETRIEVED→TODO→IMPLEMENTING→IMPLEMENTED→TESTING→TESTED→PUSHING→PUSHED→REVIEWING→REVIEWED.

Pantheon-Lens dashboard launched on port 7878. Verified Pantheon-Lens.ps1 located at .github/scripts/Pantheon-Lens.ps1. Coordination directories (.github/pantheon-temp/, .github/pantheon-worklog/) verified. Dashboard running and monitoring live coordination files.

Retrieved GitHub issue GH-88 (Github button and Documentation button should open in Default Browser of OS not in Electron application window) from https://github.com/akineralkan/fetchy/issues/88. Issue description covers goal (open in OS browser), context (Electron default behavior), and implementation notes (use shell module, expose via contextBridge, handle anchors and buttons, ensure security compliance). Created entry in jira-items.md with status TODO. Registered to Pantheon pipeline.

Implemented GH-88: GitHub and Documentation buttons now open in OS default browser. No main process changes needed (IPC already wired). Fixed 2 files: (1) src/App.tsx - replaced window.open() with window.electronAPI?.openExternalUrl() for Documentation and GitHub toolbar buttons. (2) src/components/AboutModal.tsx - converted 17 external anchor tags to button elements with openExternalUrl() onClick handlers (GitHub Repo, Docs, License, Contributors, 13 open-source dep links). TypeScript: 0 errors. Tests: 2275 passed, 9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures (unrelated). ESLint: clean.

Tested GH-88: wrote 17 new unit tests for external URL button behavior. App.test.tsx (6 tests): Documentation and GitHub toolbar buttons call openExternalUrl with correct URLs, no window.open used, graceful fallback when electronAPI absent. AboutModal.test.tsx (11 tests): GitHub Repository/Documentation/MIT License/Contributors buttons correct, all 13 OPEN_SOURCE_DEPS dep buttons correct, no <a href=> anchor tags present, graceful fallback. Full suite: 2291 passed, 9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures unrelated to GH-88. No regressions.

Performed Git operations for GH-88 (Github button and Documentation button should open in Default Browser of OS). Created feature branch feat/gh-88-external-browser-links from main. Staged 4 modified files: src/App.tsx, src/components/AboutModal.tsx, test/components/App.test.tsx, test/components/AboutModal.test.tsx. Committed with message: feat(links): open GitHub and Documentation links in OS default browser (#88). Commit hash: fb4bfea. Pushed branch to origin. PR ready at https://github.com/akineralkan/fetchy/pull/new/feat/gh-88-external-browser-links. Updated jira-items.md with status PUSHED, branch, and commit information. Updated agent-states.md to IDLE.

action: AI code review completed for GH-88 (Github/Docs buttons open in OS default browser). Branch: feat/gh-88-external-browser-links, Commit: fb4bfea. Reviewed: src/App.tsx, src/components/AboutModal.tsx, electron/main.js (open-external-url IPC), electron/preload.js, test/components/App.test.tsx, test/components/AboutModal.test.tsx. Verdict: Approve with Minor Suggestions. Findings: 0 blocking, 5 minor (IPC try/catch, URL length cap, UpdateModal dev-mode window.open, aria-hidden icons, icon size inconsistency + URL casing). 3 positives (IPC URL validation, comprehensive tests, minimal clean implementation). Status set to REVIEWED.

topic: Full Pantheon cycle for GH-88: GitHub/Documentation buttons open in OS default browser. Coordinated Hermes (retrieval), Prometheus (implementation), Themis (17 new tests, 2291 passing), Charon (branch pushed commit fb4bfea), Metis (Approve with Minor Suggestions).

Full Pantheon cycle for GH-88: GitHub/Documentation buttons open in OS default browser. Coordinated Hermes (retrieval), Prometheus (implementation), Themis (17 new tests 2291 passing), Charon (branch feat/gh-88-external-browser-links pushed commit fb4bfea), Metis (Approve with Minor Suggestions 0 blocking 5 minor).

summary: Verified GH-88 implementation already complete: electron/preload.js exposes openExternalUrl via contextBridge; electron/main.js open-external-url IPC handler calls shell.openExternal() with URL validation; src/App.tsx GitHub and Documentation buttons use window.electronAPI?.openExternalUrl(); src/components/AboutModal.tsx all external links use same pattern. TypeScript: clean. Tests: 2291 passed, 9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures unrelated to GH-88, 6 GH-88-specific App.test.tsx tests all passed.

Tested GH-88: confirmed implementation of openExternalUrl in preload.js, main.js IPC handler, App.tsx (Documentation + GitHub buttons), and AboutModal.tsx (all 17 external links). Verified 17 GH-88 tests already present (6 in App.test.tsx, 11 in AboutModal.test.tsx). All 17 GH-88 tests passed. Full suite: 2291 passed, 9 pre-existing HistoryPanel failures (unrelated to GH-88). Zero regressions. Task status: TESTED.
